title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
quote: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
about: Atomic Habits offers a practical framework for building good habits, breaking bad ones, and improving through small repeated actions.
takeaway: Focus on your systems and identity, not just the end goals.
review: A practical and powerful book about how small changes compound over time. It helped me think differently about consistency, discipline, and personal growth.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
quote: When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it.
about: A philosophical novel about Santiago, a shepherd boy who follows his dream and discovers lessons about destiny, faith, and listening to his heart.
takeaway: Follow your heart and trust the journey.
review: A beautiful and gentle story that feels meaningful without being complicated. It reminded me that the journey matters as much as the destination.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
quote: To produce at your peak level you need to work for extended periods with full concentration on a single task.
about: Deep Work argues that the ability to focus without distraction is a superpower in our increasingly competitive and noisy modern economy.
takeaway: Schedule deep work blocks and eliminate shallow distractions.
review: A game-changing productivity guide that helped me structure my coding sessions. It provides actionable strategies to cultivate deep focus and produce high-quality work.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
quote: Nothing in life is as important as you think it is, while you are thinking about it.
about: Kahneman explains the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 (fast, intuitive, emotional) and System 2 (slow, deliberative, logical).
takeaway: Recognize cognitive biases and invoke slower, logical thinking for key decisions.
review: A dense but incredibly rewarding exploration of human cognitive fallibility. It completely shifts how you view your own intuitive judgments and choices.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
quote: What important truth do very few people agree with you on?
about: Thiel presents a philosophy of building new creations from zero to one rather than adding to what already exists from one to n.
takeaway: Create a unique monopoly by doing something completely new rather than copying.
review: Extremely thought-provoking and contrarian. Even if you don't agree with every point, it challenges standard assumptions about competition and innovation.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
quote: You could never convince a monkey to give you a banana by promising him limitless bananas after death in monkey heaven.
about: Harari surveys the history of humankind from the evolutionary emergence of Homo sapiens to the present day, exploring our shared myths.
takeaway: Human cooperation is built on shared myths and imagined realities.
review: Breathtaking in scope and narrative. It weaves history, science, and philosophy to explain how we became the rulers of planet Earth.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
quote: Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares.
about: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that describes the principles, patterns, and practices of writing clean, maintainable code.
takeaway: Write code for humans first and computers second. Leave the code cleaner than you found it.
review: A absolute classic for software developers. The principles of naming, function design, and comment reduction are fundamental to growing as a professional.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
quote: Doing well with money has a little to do with how smart you are and a lot to do with how you behave.
about: Housel shares 19 short stories exploring the strange ways people think about money, demonstrating that behavior is more important than technical skill.
takeaway: Wealth is what you don't see. Control over your time is the highest dividend money pays.
review: Incredibly readable and insightful. It's less about asset allocation and more about building a healthy, stress-free relationship with personal finance.
